rednote-hilab/dots.ocr ·GitHub ⭐ 7998 Python · OCR, VLM, Open-Source-AI, Document-Layout, SVG-Parsing, Multilingual

What it is

A state-of-the-art multilingual OCR and image parsing model that converts document layout and complex graphics into structured text or SVG.

Problems solved

  • Poor performance on non-Latin scripts; loss of structure (tables/formulas) during digitization; lack of tools for converting charts into code.

How to get started

Requires Python 3.12 and PyTorch 2.7; deploy via vLLM for best performance using 'vllm serve rednote-hilab/dots.ocr-1.5'.

Detailed report

1. Positioning

rednote-hilab/dots.ocr is positioned as a a state-of-the-art multilingual ocr and image parsing model that converts document layout and complex graphics into structured text or svg. It aims to provide developers with a robust, Python-based solution for multilingual document layout parsing in a single vision-language model.

2. Pain Points & Advantages

Traditional methods in this domain often suffer from Poor performance on non-Latin scripts; loss of structure (tables/formulas) during digitization; lack of tools for converting charts into code.. rednote-hilab/dots.ocr addresses this by a state-of-the-art multilingual ocr and image parsing model that converts document layout and complex graphics into structured text or svg, offering a significant performance boost and better developer ergonomics compared to existing alternatives.

3. Technical Architecture

The project is built using Python, adopting a modular and scalable design. It leverages modern industry standards to ensure compatibility across different environments, focusing on efficiency and ease of integration.

4. Core Implementation

Main logic revolves around multilingual document layout parsing in a single vision-language model. The implementation utilizes Python's strengths to provide a state-of-the-art multilingual ocr and image parsing model that converts document layout and complex graphics into structured text or svg, ensuring high reliability and performance under load.

5. Code Organization

The repository is structured logically, with core functionality separated from utilities and examples. This organization facilitates easy navigation and contribution for developers looking to extend the tool's capabilities.

6. Quick Start Suggestion

To get started, requires python 3.12 and pytorch 2.7; deploy via vllm for best performance using 'vllm serve rednote-hilab/dots.ocr-1.5'. This will allow you to quickly evaluate the tool and integrate it into your existing development workflow.

7. Summary

An essential, Python-powered utility that streamlines multilingual document layout parsing in a single vision-language model; it effectively tackles Poor performance on non-Latin scripts; loss of structure (tables/formulas) during digitization; lack of tools for converting charts into code. while maintaining a high standard of code quality and usability.
